All output watermarked with SynthID to identify AI-generated speech and prevent misinformation.
why_it_matters: Developers gain fine-grained control over speech generation, enabling more natural, localized AI applications at scale.

original_title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
headline: Google DeepMind releases Gemma 4 open models with advanced reasoning
bullets: Four sizes: 2B, 4B, 26B MoE, 31B dense; 31B ranks #3 on Arena AI leaderboard
Native multimodal support: video, images, audio; up to 256K context window
Apache 2.0 license; runs on Android devices, laptops, and enterprise hardware
why_it_matters: Developers can deploy frontier-class reasoning locally without proprietary restrictions or cloud dependency.

original_title: Reimagining the mouse pointer for the AI era
headline: Google DeepMind reimagines mouse pointer with AI context awareness
bullets: AI-enabled pointer understands visual context without detailed prompts, powered by Gemini.
Rolling out in Chrome and Googlebook laptop; users point and speak instead of typing prompts.
Four design principles: maintain workflow, capture context visually, enable natural shorthand, convert pixels to actionable entities.
why_it_matters: Eliminates friction in AI interactions by bringing capabilities directly into existing workflows rather than forcing users into separate AI tools.

original_title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
headline: Google releases Gemini 3.1 Flash Live audio model for natural voice interactions
bullets: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live scores 90.8% on ComplexFuncBench Audio for multi-step function calling tasks.
Model available via Gemini Live API for developers, enterprises, and 200+ countries via Search Live.
All audio output watermarked with SynthID to detect AI-generated content and prevent misinformation.
why_it_matters: Developers can build more reliable voice agents handling complex tasks with improved tone understanding and lower latency.
